tasks.title: Add .laps/.gitignore regression test
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec change tidy-rally-runtime-data-storage (specs/tooling-distribution). The stray .laps/.gitignore was already removed and laps.json already tracked as of commit 33d288e. This lap adds a regression test to prevent recurrence.

Outcome: A regression test proves rally never writes a .laps/.gitignore. Grep confirms no code path creates one.

Files & scope: Add a test near laps.InstallHooks (internal, in the rally repo) asserting no .gitignore is created under .laps/. Do not touch .laps/hooks.json or the hooks/ dir.

Acceptance: New test passes; grep confirms no code path writes a .laps/.gitignore.

tasks.title: Centralise .rally/ path construction behind helpers
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec change tidy-rally-runtime-data-storage (specs/store, design Decision 1). Paths are built ad-hoc with filepath.Join(..., ".rally", ...) across packages. This lap establishes the path contract that every later lap builds on; no behavior change yet.

Outcome: A single set of helpers (e.g. store.RallyDir, store.StateDir(workspaceDir), store.SummaryPath) own all .rally/ path building, and existing call sites use them. Files still resolve to their current locations (the move happens in the next lap).

Files & scope: internal/store/{store,cache}.go, internal/progress/ (store.go, runstate.go), internal/cli/hooks.go, internal/relay/runner.go (current_task.md at ~698). Add helpers in internal/store (or a small paths file) and route joins through them.

Design: Keep helpers dependency-light so laps/other packages need not import heavy deps. Do not yet introduce state/ — just funnel the joins so the relocation lap is a one-line change per helper.

Acceptance: `go build ./...` and `go test ./...` pass; grep shows no remaining ad-hoc `".rally"` joins outside the helpers.

tasks.title: Move machine data into .rally/state/; local windowing; gitignore+README templates
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec change tidy-rally-runtime-data-storage (specs/store MODIFIED, design Decisions 1-2). Builds on the path helpers. BREAKING: machine data leaves the top level and is no longer git-tracked.

Outcome: tries/relays/agent_status/messages.jsonl, hook-audit.jsonl, run-state.json, and current_task.md all resolve under .rally/state/. The .rally/.gitignore template is a single `state/` line. Windowing truncates in place locally (no git commit). README template describes the new layout and drops the false git-tracked-JSONL claim.

Files & scope: internal/store/{store,cache}.go (state/ paths + truncation), internal/progress/runstate.go, internal/cli/hooks.go, internal/relay/runner.go, cmd/rally/main.go (gitignore template ~355 + README template ~398).

Design: Replace commit-then-truncate-to-git with in-place truncate keeping most-recent N (200 tries / 50 relays / 500 agent_status; pending messages exempt). No git operations in the windowing path. Migration of EXISTING files is a later lap — here just make new writes land in state/ and create the dir on write.

Acceptance: `go build ./...`; fresh `rally init` in a temp dir produces .rally/.gitignore containing only `state/` and writes records under .rally/state/; existing store tests updated to new paths pass.

tasks.title: VERIFY: gitignore fix, path helpers, state/ relocation
tasks.description:
Use the openspec-verify-change skill against tidy-rally-runtime-data-storage, then focus the report on the work so far: the .laps/.gitignore regression test, centralised path helpers, and the .rally/state/ relocation with local windowing.

Before diffing: identify the intended merge target (ask the user, or infer from PR metadata/repo docs/branch config/recent history) and identify the first lap/try in this batch; treat earlier branch work as pre-existing baseline.

Checks: `git diff <target>...HEAD` for the relocation; confirm no ad-hoc `".rally"` joins remain; confirm the gitignore template is just `state/`; confirm windowing no longer runs git; `go build ./...` and `go test ./...`. Report findings first. For substantive gaps, `laps add head` a focused fix lap rather than fixing it here (only tiny one-liners allowed). Do not rewrite git history.

tasks.title: Replace progress.yaml with append-only summary.jsonl
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec change tidy-rally-runtime-data-storage (specs/run-summary, design Decision 2). summary.jsonl becomes the sole tracked top-level data file.

Outcome: internal/progress writes one JSON line per finalized run/handoff to .rally/summary.jsonl, preserving the RunEntry/HandoffEntry shape and the AppendRunEntry signature (so runner.go:1460 is untouched). progress.yaml is no longer written; history_window trimming and YAML read/write paths are removed. The ProgressLog struct, LoadProgress, and SaveProgress functions are removed. internal/progress/cli.go (which drives the `rally progress` command) is updated to use the new JSONL-based API.

Files & scope: internal/progress/store.go (reimplement as JSONL appender), internal/progress/cli.go (update to use new API). Do not change the AppendRunEntry call site in internal/relay/runner.go.

Design: Append-only, no rewrite/merge. Keep records small (summary text + ids + timestamps). RFC3339 updated_at.

Acceptance: `go build ./...`; finalizing a run appends a parseable JSON line to .rally/summary.jsonl; no progress.yaml is created; `tail -1 .rally/summary.jsonl | jq .` works.

tasks.title: One-time idempotent .rally/ migration
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec change tidy-rally-runtime-data-storage (specs/store ADDED: Runtime data layout migration, design Decision 5). High-risk: moves real user data.

Outcome: On `rally init` and lazily on first store write, an idempotent migration: creates .rally/state/, moves legacy flat files in WITHOUT overwriting existing targets, converts legacy progress.yaml -> summary.jsonl (one JSON line per recent_runs entry) removing progress.yaml only after a successful write, and removes legacy .rally/batches/, the legacy top-level .rally/relays/ log dir, and .rally/config.toml.bak (if present — these are legacy artifacts that may not exist in all repos). Re-running is a no-op.

Files & scope: cmd/rally/main.go (runInit ~332) and/or a migration helper in internal/store or internal/progress; invoke lazily from the first store write.

Design: Move-don't-copy with existence checks; never clobber an existing state/ target; parse progress.yaml recent_runs and emit summary.jsonl lines; guard every step so a partially-migrated dir converges cleanly. No git history rewriting.

Acceptance: `go build ./...`; manual run against a fixture legacy .rally/ migrates correctly and a second run changes nothing (tests come in a later lap).

tasks.title: VERIFY: summary.jsonl and migration
tasks.description:
Use the openspec-verify-change skill against tidy-rally-runtime-data-storage, then focus on the summary.jsonl replacement and the one-time migration (the highest-risk slice).

Before diffing: confirm the merge target with the user/PR/repo signals and the batch's first lap; earlier work is pre-existing baseline.

Checks: build a fixture legacy .rally/ (flat jsonl + progress.yaml + batches/ + relays/ + config.toml.bak) and run migration twice — verify correct moves, no-overwrite behavior, progress.yaml->summary.jsonl conversion, legacy cleanup, and full idempotency. Confirm progress.yaml is never re-created on subsequent runs. Confirm internal/progress/cli.go works with the new API. `go build ./...`. Report findings first; `laps add head` focused fixes for substantive gaps (tiny one-liners only here). Do not rewrite git history.

tasks.title: Telemetry sink scaffolding (Sentry, opt-in, flush)
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec change tidy-rally-runtime-data-storage (specs/telemetry, design Decision 3). New external dependency and new pattern.

Outcome: A telemetry.Sink interface with a default no-op impl and a Sentry-backed impl that activates ONLY when a DSN is configured. Config precedence: SENTRY_DSN env overrides [telemetry] sentry_dsn in config.toml; RALLY_TELEMETRY=0 force-disables. Sink is initialised once in cmd/rally/main.go with `defer sentry.Flush(2s)` before exit; flush is bounded so an unreachable network never hangs exit.

Files & scope: new internal/telemetry package; go.mod (add github.com/getsentry/sentry-go); config struct + config.toml template (add [telemetry] sentry_dsn) in cmd/rally + internal/cli/config.go; cmd/rally/main.go init+flush.

Design: Keep the Sink interface narrow (emit-try, relay/run/try spans, capture-failure) so an OTel impl could swap in later. No-op must be allocation-cheap and never touch the network. Do not wire emit points yet (next lap).

Acceptance: `go build ./...` and `go test ./...` pass; with no DSN nothing is sent and there is no network access; RALLY_TELEMETRY=0 disables even when a DSN is present.

tasks.title: Wire telemetry emit points, tagging, failures, scrubber
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec change tidy-rally-runtime-data-storage (specs/telemetry). Builds on the sink scaffolding.

Outcome: Per-try structured log emitted at store.AppendTry (runner.go:1045); relay/run/try modelled as a trace with spans; recognized failures (non-zero exit, panic, agent-exited-without-finalizing, laps-done-as-text, lap-integrity violations) captured as Sentry Issues. Route fallback (rotating to a backup runner) logged as a common recovery event, NOT an Issue. Every event tagged relay_id/run_id/try_id/role/runner/repo/lap_ids. A before_send scrubber NEVER ships current_task.md contents or full transcripts (summaries + metadata only).

Files & scope: internal/relay/runner.go (emit at try append ~1045 + relay start/end), internal/telemetry (tagging helpers, before_send scrubber). Avoid passing the 120KB current_task.md into any event payload.

Design: Tags drive filterability (replacing jq over committed jsonl). Scrubber is defense-in-depth even though we already avoid sending the prompt. Relay = transaction, run = span, try = child span; trace id correlates to relay id.

Acceptance: `go build ./...`; with a dummy/disabled sink the emit calls are exercised in tests later; manual review confirms no current_task.md or transcript bytes can reach an event payload.

tasks.title: VERIFY: telemetry sink and instrumentation
tasks.description:
Use the openspec-verify-change skill against tidy-rally-runtime-data-storage, then focus on the telemetry capability: opt-in gating, tagging, failure capture, scrubbing, and bounded flush.

Before diffing: confirm merge target and the batch's first lap; earlier work is baseline.

Checks: confirm no-DSN = no-op/no network; SENTRY_DSN overrides config; RALLY_TELEMETRY=0 kills it; every emit path carries the required tags; the before_send scrubber removes current_task.md/transcripts; flush is bounded; route fallback is logged as common event not Issue. `go build ./...` and `go test ./...`. Report findings first; `laps add head` focused fixes for substantive gaps (tiny one-liners only here). Do not rewrite git history.

tasks.title: Bundle laps alongside rally: install.sh, rally update extension, version check
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec change tidy-rally-runtime-data-storage (specs/tooling-distribution, design Decision 4 + AGENTS.md release process). Distribution/release-sensitive.

Outcome: install.sh fetches and installs a compatible laps binary next to rally (from https://github.com/mitchell-wallace/rally). The existing `rally update` command (cmd/rally/main.go:505) is extended to also upgrade laps. A startup minimum-laps-version check warns (not hard-fail) and advises `rally update` when laps is too old. .laps/ stays a separate top-level dir; laps must not read .rally/.

Files & scope: install.sh, cmd/rally (extend existing update command ~505 + version-check at startup), wherever laps hooks are installed (laps.InstallHooks call in main.go ~169 for reference). Respect the auto-tag/GoReleaser release flow described in AGENTS.md (do not hand-tag).

Design: Pin/declare a minimum laps version the hooks contract needs. `rally update` should be the single entry point that re-runs the install/upgrade for both binaries. Keep laps independently installable.

Acceptance: `go build ./...`; `rally update --help` exists and mentions laps; running rally with an old/missing laps prints a warning and continues; install.sh references both binaries.

tasks.title: Tests: migration, store/state, summary.jsonl
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec change tidy-rally-runtime-data-storage (tasks 8.1-8.3). Follow existing table-test patterns in internal/store and internal/progress.

Outcome: Tests covering (a) migration on a fixture legacy .rally/ — flat->state move, no-overwrite, progress.yaml->summary.jsonl conversion, legacy cleanup (batches/, relays/ log dir, config.toml.bak — if present), idempotency; (b) store/cache read+write under .rally/state/ and local-only truncation windowing; (c) summary.jsonl append shape and that progress.yaml is never written.

Files & scope: *_test.go in internal/store, internal/progress, and cmd/rally as appropriate. Use t.TempDir fixtures.

Acceptance: `go test ./internal/store/... ./internal/progress/... ./cmd/...` passes; tests fail if migration is non-idempotent or if progress.yaml reappears.

tasks.title: Tests: telemetry, prompt-size, commit history, distribution
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec change tidy-rally-runtime-data-storage (tasks 8.4-8.7).

Outcome: Tests covering (a) the telemetry sink: no-op without DSN, RALLY_TELEMETRY=0 kill switch, SENTRY_DSN-over-config precedence, required tags present on emitted events, before_send scrubber drops current_task.md, route fallback logged as common event not Issue; (b) prompt-size fields: total + per-source breakdown present on the try log, Issue criteria (infra failure + relay stall → Issue; agent-class retry → no Issue); (c) try commit history: multiple commits retained in order, single commit as one-element list, CommitHash backward compat set to last element; (d) distribution: `rally update` wiring and the min-version check warning vs silent paths.

Files & scope: *_test.go in internal/telemetry, internal/store, and cmd/rally. Use a fake/in-memory transport or a captured-event hook rather than real network calls.

Acceptance: `go test ./internal/telemetry/... ./internal/store/... ./cmd/...` passes; a test asserts a current_task.md payload never survives before_send; a test asserts the version check warns on old laps and is silent on a compatible version; a test asserts CommitHistory ordering and CommitHash backward compat.

tasks.title: Docs and rollout: README/AGENTS, skill refs, VERSION bump
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec change tidy-rally-runtime-data-storage (tasks 9.1-9.3).

Outcome: Docs reflect the new layout: AGENTS.md/README.md and skill references (test-driving-rally, post-relay-review including post-relay-review/references/prayer-app-2.md) that mention progress.yaml or flat .rally/*.jsonl paths now point to .rally/state/ + summary.jsonl. Telemetry opt-in is documented (DSN config, SENTRY_DSN, RALLY_TELEMETRY kill switch, what is/ is not sent) along with `rally update`. internal/buildinfo/VERSION is bumped per the release process in AGENTS.md (patch bump unless told otherwise).

Files & scope: README.md, AGENTS.md, .rally/README.md template (if not already done), .claude/skills/test-driving-rally/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/post-relay-review/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/post-relay-review/references/prayer-app-2.md, internal/buildinfo/VERSION.

Acceptance: grep finds no stale progress.yaml / flat-jsonl path guidance; telemetry + update docs present; VERSION incremented.

tasks.title: VERIFY: full change
tasks.description:
Final verification for tidy-rally-runtime-data-storage. Run the complete openspec-verify-change skill against the change and inspect the whole diff.

Before diffing: confirm the intended merge target with the user (or infer from PR metadata/repo docs/branch config/recent history) and identify the first lap/try in this batch; treat earlier branch work as pre-existing baseline, flag but do not remove it unless the user asks.

Checks across the whole outcome: `git diff <target>...HEAD`; `go build ./...` and `go test ./...`; confirm tracked .rally/ is limited to config.toml/agents/README.md/summary.jsonl with state/ gitignored; migration idempotent; telemetry opt-in and scrubbed; laps bundled with extended `rally update` + version check; regression test proves no .laps/.gitignore is written; CommitHistory field on TryRecord with CommitHash backward compat; route fallback logged as common event not Issue. `openspec validate tidy-rally-runtime-data-storage --strict`. Report findings first; `laps add head` focused laps for substantive gaps (tiny one-liners only here). Do not rewrite git history.
